_metadata.honesty_note: R3 is tier red in plausibility.md. The only quantitative claim is 'required potential gradient ~ c H_0 ~ 2e-8 m/s^2'. The CMB dipole (3.36 mK) and peculiar velocity (369 km/s) are quoted from standard cosmology. This verification checks the gradient claim and the self-consistency of the dipole / velocity pair.

CMB_dipole_mK.source: Planck 2018; standard CMB dipole amplitude (kinematic interpretation)

peculiar_velocity_km_s.source: Planck 2018 Table 9; solar-barycentric velocity relative to CMB rest frame

potential_gradient_claim_m_s2.source: R3 plausibility.md; claimed value of d Phi/dr needed to give observed H0 at Hubble scale

Secrest_2021_quasar_dipole_sigma.source: Secrest et al. 2021, ApJL 908, L51; quasar/galaxy-count dipole vs CMB dipole misalignment
